# Prove the BigData Neural Link probe and erase diagnostics

## Context

Epic #15184 needs one reproducible, blind local interoperability journey after the ingress and exact-projection leaves are complete. The approved source contract lives in [D#15173](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/discussions/15173).

Live duplicate sweep: checked the latest 20 open issues, broader GitHub search, recent A2A claims, Knowledge Base tickets, and synced issue/discussion content at 2026-07-14T22:05:39.479Z; no equivalent active ticket exists. The only live search hit was the parent Epic.

**Self-selection boundary:** externally implementation-eligible. A Neo maintainer remains the blind-oracle custodian and final deletion verifier. The external client readiness receipt is a prerequisite, not a Neo sub-ticket.

## The Problem

A transport handshake alone does not prove that a fresh external agent can interpret live Neo structure honestly. The proof must establish three things together:

1. the external client can use the standard URL transport and exact three-operation profile;
2. its result matches a precommitted BigData structural oracle without being taught the answer;
3. all raw probe diagnostics are erased after joint review.

The diagnostic boundary is broader than the original shorthand suggested. Current code can write:

- raw Neural Link args/results into SQLite `nl_action_log` at the `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH` destination;
- rotating `nl-server-*` files under `NEO_NL_LOG_PATH`;
- detached Bridge stdout/stderr into `neural-link-bridge-stdio.log` under that same log root.

A cleanup step that deletes only `nl-server-*` leaves a raw Bridge sink behind. The journey must isolate and delete the entire temporary root, not a guessed filename subset.

## The Architectural Reality

- `ai/services/neural-link/RecorderService.mjs` opens the configured Memory Core SQLite path, enables WAL, and stores `tool`, `args`, `result`, success, duration, and app metadata.
- `ai/mcp/server/neural-link/config.mjs` maps `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH` and `NEO_NL_LOG_PATH`.
- The shared logger writes `nl-server-*` files regardless of debug mode.
- `ai/services/neural-link/ConnectionService.mjs#getBridgeStdioLogPath` writes `neural-link-bridge-stdio.log` into the configured Neural Link log root when it spawns the Bridge.
- The approved profile exposes `healthcheck`, `get_worker_topology`, and lean `get_component_tree` at explicit depth 2 for the oracle journey.
- The public BigData app supplies a deterministic root/direct-child structure; one explicit App Worker session is required so target selection cannot drift.

## The Fix

Add one reference journey that creates a unique temporary directory, places the SQLite database inside it through `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH`, uses the same directory for `NEO_NL_LOG_PATH`, starts the local server/Bridge and public BigData app, and connects through the standard MCP client using the output of #15185 and #15186.

Before external execution, a Neo maintainer canonicalizes the expected structure as whitespace-free UTF-8 JSON with fixed property order:

`{"rootClass":"…","directChildren":[{"index":0,"className":"…"}]}`

Direct children remain in declared order. The custodian generates a secret 32-byte lowercase-hex salt and publishes:

`SHA-256(UTF-8(saltHex + "\n" + canonicalJson))`

After the external deliverable is frozen, reveal the salt and canonical JSON so both sides reproduce the hash.

After joint review, stop the MCP server and Bridge, close SQLite handles, capture a recursive before-manifest, delete the entire unique temporary root—including database/WAL/SHM, `nl-server-*`, `neural-link-bridge-stdio.log`, and any unexpected artifact—and publish an after-manifest proving the root is absent. Prove the default live database/log paths were never touched.

## Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback / Compatibility | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Reference journey | Ticket + committed journey entrypoint | One BigData app, one local URL client, exact profile, deterministic command | Fail before probe if preconditions are not exact | Journey guide | Exact command/config/version receipt |
| External readiness | `GENESIS_PROBE_READY` public marker | Named commit SHA/version, config, command, stable suite/CI output | Missing marker keeps this ticket blocked | Ticket prerequisite | Linked public receipt |
| Blind oracle | Neo maintainer custody + canonical JSON contract | Publish salted commitment before submission; reveal after frozen deliverable | Hash mismatch is probe failure | Journey guide | Reproduced hash by both parties |
| Raw diagnostics | Unique temp root owning both env paths | All SQLite, rotating server, Bridge stdio, and unexpected files stay inside root | Abort if any sink escapes root | Cleanup contract | Recursive before/after manifests |
| Final result | Public success/failure receipt | Root class, complete direct-child set, explicit not-inferable list, per-claim trace | Unsupported confident claim is failure | Receipt schema | Frozen deliverable + oracle comparison |

## Decision Record impact

`aligned-with ADR 0020`; no ADR amendment.

## Decision Record

`NOT_NEEDED` — consumes the local transport/projection contract and existing diagnostics paths.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Native prerequisites #15185 and #15186 are complete, and the public `GENESIS_PROBE_READY` marker links a named client commit SHA/version, trusted-loopback config, exact test command, and stable output proving POST-first JSON-RPC, `Mcp-Session-Id` roundtrip, JSON plus SSE-on-POST responses, default-deny loopback, and bearer-gated named-server opt-in.
- [ ] The journey fails before probing unless exactly one intended BigData App Worker session is available.
- [ ] The reference client connects through standard Streamable HTTP, the literal loopback URL, disposable bearer, and server-pinned `local-readonly-probe` profile.
- [ ] A Neo maintainer publishes the salted commitment before the external deliverable using the exact canonical JSON property order, declared child order, whitespace-free UTF-8 bytes, 32-byte lowercase-hex salt, newline separator, and SHA-256 formula above.
- [ ] The external deliverable is frozen before the custodian reveals salt and oracle; both parties reproduce the commitment hash.
- [ ] The deliverable names the live root class, the complete direct-child set visible at depth 2, and an explicit not-inferable list; every positive claim traces to a returned field and any unsupported confident claim is recorded as failure.
- [ ] One unique temporary root owns a database file supplied via `NEO_MEMORY_DB_PATH` and the log directory supplied via `NEO_NL_LOG_PATH`; startup aborts if any configured raw-diagnostic sink resolves outside it.
- [ ] The recursive before-manifest records relative path, type, and byte size for every artifact, including database/WAL/SHM when present, `nl-server-*`, `neural-link-bridge-stdio.log`, and any unexpected file.
- [ ] After joint review, the journey stops server and Bridge, closes database handles, deletes the entire temporary root, and publishes an after-manifest proving absence plus evidence that default live database/log paths were untouched.
- [ ] Only aggregate tool/status/duration counts, the revealed public oracle, and final success/failure receipt survive. Bearer, headers, raw args/results, raw logs, and unrevealed oracle material do not.
- [ ] The live probe stays within one session of at most two hours plus one asynchronous correction cycle; the receipt records start/end timestamps.
- [ ] One PR fully delivers the journey tooling, documentation, focused tests, and completed public receipt; it `Resolves` this leaf and only `Refs` Epic #15184.
- [ ] Changed classes/methods satisfy Contextual Completeness JSDoc and `@summary` requirements.

## Out of Scope

- Implementing the external client's transport changes inside Neo.
- Cloud/container deployment, public endpoints, PAT/OAuth/OIDC, or tenant identity.
- Mutations, reciprocal access, logs/store/source/method disclosure, or a wider tool profile.
- Retaining raw telemetry after review.

## External prerequisite satisfied — `GENESIS_PROBE_READY`

The Genesis readiness receipt is accepted at [D#15173 comment 17653193](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/discussions/15173#discussioncomment-17653193), after the original receipt audit and follow-up correction.

Canonical external artifact:

- package version: `7.9.38`;
- final commit: [`2a7e9d75d2e5cceb9d36fd0dc290c7586d9ad4c8`](https://github.com/Garrus800-stack/genesis-agent/commit/2a7e9d75d2e5cceb9d36fd0dc290c7586d9ad4c8);
- exact command: `node test/modules/v7938-mcp-streamable.contract.test.js`;
- receipt output: 5 contract tests passed and 9,190 full-suite tests passed;
- verified correction: empty, whitespace-only, non-Bearer, and empty-credential authorization values cannot open the trusted-loopback gate.

This removes the external execution prerequisite from the readiness ledger. The native `blockedBy` gates on #15185 and #15186 remain unchanged; no journey execution starts before both are complete.

## Ticket intake — `valid-as-written`

### Reality classification

- **Ticket age:** created `2026-07-14T22:05:40Z`; updated `2026-07-15T19:56:56Z`.
- **Bot stale-band:** `pre-stale`; neither `stale` nor `no auto close` is present.
- **Currency / successor risk:** current `origin/dev` contains merged PR #15214 for #15185 and merged PR #15224 for #15186. Those same-day successors unblock this leaf; they do not supersede it. The accepted `GENESIS_PROBE_READY` receipt remains linked in `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABKR0Bkw`.
- **Current-source falsifiers:** `createLocalBearerLaunchContract()` supplies the disposable loopback bearer contract; `TransportService` binds local-bearer mode to literal `127.0.0.1`; the Neural Link OpenAPI authority now pins `local-readonly-probe` to exactly three tools and depth `<= 2`; Recorder, server logger, and Bridge stdio sinks still resolve through the two ticketed temp-root paths.
- **ADR successor risk:** `adr-aligned` with ADR 0020; no amendment is needed. ADR 0019 remains binding during implementation: the journey will inject environment into child entrypoints and consume config at its owning use sites, without runtime config mutation or a second env-derivation layer.
- **Duplicate / close-link sweep:** the live semantic GitHub search returns only this leaf and parent Epic #15184; no open or merged PR closes #15187.
- **Epic entry gate:** the prior structured Epic Review is [recorded on #15184](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/15184#issuecomment-4974669103).
- **Contract readiness:** the Contract Ledger covers the reference command, external readiness, blind oracle, raw-diagnostic ownership, cleanup proof, and final receipt. Its live surface anchors match current source.

### Structural pre-flight

Considered `ai/scripts/diagnostics/` and `ai/examples/`. The journey is a deterministic one-shot operator diagnostic, matching `mcpHealthcheck.mjs` plus the temp-root/cleanup discipline tested by `bootstrapCodexSandbox.mjs`; it is not an Agent OS teaching demo, daemon, or reusable service. Chosen homes:

- `ai/scripts/diagnostics/genesisProbe.mjs`
- `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/diagnostics/genesisProbe.spec.mjs`
- journey documentation under `learn/agentos/tooling/`

The Structural Inventory already covers this role, so no map or ADR addition is required.

### ROI

Positive: this is the final 1:1 source-Discussion proof for Epic #15184, reuses the two merged primitives, and closes the highest-risk privacy claim by proving whole-root diagnostic erasure. The bounded journey is cheaper than leaving transport compatibility and cleanup as prose-only assumptions.

**Verdict:** `valid-as-written`; ready for assignment, branch creation, and implementation.
